Antimicrobial susceptibility testing (Broth microdilution method) 2025. Jul.
Rakuno Gakuen University Veterinary AMR Center, NVAL, MAFF Measurement method Dilution method Agar dilution Broth dilution method Test tube broth dilution method Broth microdilution method Diffusion method Disk diffusion method Antimicrobial susceptibility test method E-test Minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) measurement 2 Reagents and equipment prepared by the broth microdilution method 1 9 8 3 7 6 5 4 2 10 11 ①Microtiter plate ②Lid ③Reading mirror ④Tip ⑤12 or 8 channel Pipette ⑥Micropipette ⑦Tested strains ⑧Reservoir ⑨Cation adjuseted Muller-Hinton Broth ⑩3 mL sterile saline ⑪Turbidity standard solution 3 Adjustment turbidity of bacterial suspension ・Pick a test organism on an overnight agar culture ・With 3 mL of sterile saline suspension (Adjust the turbidity so that it becomes turbid between Mcfarland standard turbidity solution 0.5 and 1.) 4 Add 0.025 mL (25 μL) of the previously prepared bacterial suspension in ddition to 12mL Muller-Hinton broth Mix evenly and use this as the bacterial suspension for inoculation.
Adjustment of inoculating bacterial suspension 5 Inoculation and culture of the bacterial suspension ・Transfer inoculum into reservoir ・Inoculate the wells of the microtiter plate with 100 μL of the bacterial suspension using an 8- or 12-channel pipette fitted with tips, starting with the wells with the lowest drug concentration.
※Remove the microtiter plate from the refrigerator beforehand and bring it back to room temperature.
Since the antimicrobials is dry and stuck to the bottom of the wells of the dry plate, be careful to avoid touch with the tips during inoculation.
・After inoculation, cover the plates and incubate under the culture conditions specified for each bacterial species. (When culturing, the number of stacked plates should be limited to a maximum of three.) 6 Evaluation Positive Negative Positive Positive [Positive Growth Criteria] ・Turbidity or precipitation of 1 mm or more in diameter is observed ・Two or more precipitates are found even if the diameter of the precipitates is less than 1mm [Negative Growth Criteria] ・No visible turbidity or precipitation ・1 precipitate less than 1 mm in diameter, even if present 7 ≧１mm <１mm Turbidity Judgment example 8 Drug dilution concentration (μg/mL) 0.5 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 Cont rol Growth of bacteria + + + + + ---+ Minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) MIC 16 μg/mL MIC >64 μg/mL MIC ≤0.5 μg/mL 9 Drug dilution concentration (μg/mL) 0.5 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 Cont rol Growth of bacteria + ＋ + + + + + + + Drug dilution concentration (μg/mL) 0.5 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 Cont rol Growth of bacteria ---------Drug dilution concentration Drug dilution concentration (μg/mL) 0.5 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 Cont rol Growth of bacteria + -+ -+ ---+ Drug dilution concentration Drug dilution concentration (μg/mL) 0.5 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 Cont rol Growth of bacteria + + + + + ----Re-test required In the absence of control growth Growing (skipping) in excess of concentration 10 For QC strains ・When the micro dilution method is performed, the MICs of the QC strain (according to CLSI) are also measured.
・The MIC of the QC strain must be within the range of the QC reference value.
11
